title: 30o: OpenSSL as SHA-256 oracle in tests
description: The SHA-256 test vector (SHA256_ABC = ba7816bf...) is derived from 'echo -n abc | openssl dgst -sha256', which is independent of the sha2 crate used in production. This satisfies the project's independent-oracle rule: a test that hashes with sha2 and verifies with sha2 proves only internal consistency, not spec conformance. Using OpenSSL as the oracle means the test would catch a regression if the sha2 crate were swapped for a broken implementation, or if compute_sha256_hex introduced a bug in its hex-encoding path.
close_reason: decision record: OpenSSL oracle is correct approach
